name: Eli Lilly and Company v. Medtronic, Inc.
question: Are activities that would normally constitute patent infringement excused if they are used to develop and submit information to the Food and Drug Administration to obtain marketing approval for a medical device?
description: A case in which the Court decided whether activities that would normally constitute patent infringement are excused if they are used to develop and submit information to the Food and Drug Administration to obtain marketing approval for a medical device.
